title: System-tests w/ netavark: not ok 298 podman network reload
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When `netavark` and `aardvark-dns` binaries are placed in `/usr/local/libexec/podman/` and no CNI networks are configured, Podman will operate with it's new network stack.  This is causing the `podman network reload` test to fail with an error.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Place `netavark` and `aardvark-dns` binaries in `/usr/local/libexec/podman/`

2. Remove all CNI configuration

3. Execute podman system tests with `$NETWORK_BACKEND=netavark`.

**Describe the results you received:**

[See the log from CI.](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5138206667571200/html/sys-podman-fedora-35-root-host-netavark.log.html)
```
...cut...
         # # podman inspect 9646bdba434b65fa955f424d11d4c0d4f1d4f91040433099e706a1c01979e714 --format {{(index .NetworkSettings.Networks "podman").IPAddress}}
         # 10.88.1.187
         # # podman inspect 9646bdba434b65fa955f424d11d4c0d4f1d4f91040433099e706a1c01979e714 --format {{(index .NetworkSettings.Networks "podman").MacAddress}}
         # 7e:94:48:3d:bd:9d
         # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #| FAIL: curl did not timeout, status code: 0
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

All system tests should pass in CI.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Built from source

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

aardvark-dns & netavark binaries came from upstream CI builds on the date this issue was filed.

Ref: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13024

comments.body: This requires a small tweak to the iptables command in the test. It has to flush the netavark rule and not CNI.

@cevich feel free to apply this diff:
```diff
$ git diff
diff --git a/test/system/500-networking.bats b/test/system/500-networking.bats
index 5a721c965..4e787455a 100644
--- a/test/system/500-networking.bats
+++ b/test/system/500-networking.bats
@@ -256,8 +256,12 @@ load helpers
 
     # rootless cannot modify iptables
     if ! is_rootless; then
-        # flush the CNI iptables here
-        run iptables -t nat -F CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT
+        # flush the port forwarding iptable rule here
+        chain="CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT"
+        if is_netavark; then
+            chain="NETAVARK-HOSTPORT-DNAT"
+        fi
+        run iptables -t nat -F "$chain"
 
         # check that we cannot curl (timeout afte
```
